patterns.name: ignore-previous
patterns.description: Classic override prelude — tells the model to throw out its current instructions.
patterns.source: OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications 2026 (LLM01: Agent Goal Hijack)

patterns.name: system-tag
patterns.description: Fake system tag — embeds content pretending to be a system instruction turn.
patterns.source: OWASP Top 10 Agentic 2026; arXiv:2601.17548 (structural role confusion)

patterns.name: instruction-tag
patterns.description: Fake instruction tag — less common than system-tag but same family.

patterns.name: new-instructions-heading
patterns.description: Markdown heading pretending to introduce a new authoritative block. Structural red flag.
patterns.source: Snyk ToxicSkills 2026 audit — most common skill-marketplace payload

patterns.name: system-heading
patterns.description: Heading that appears to assert system-level authority. Must be treated as content per Markdown guardrail.

patterns.name: unicode-tag-block
patterns.description: Three or more consecutive Unicode tag-channel characters (U+E0000-U+E007F). Used for invisible instruction injection.

patterns.name: base64-blob-suspicious
patterns.description: Long base64-like blob (>=60 chars). Often a disguised payload; benign uses exist so severity is medium.
patterns.source: OWASP Top 10 Agentic 2026 (LLM02: Tool Misuse — opaque payload smuggling)

patterns.name: comment-and-control-marker
patterns.description: Signature from the Comment-and-Control attack demonstrated Jan 2026 against Claude Code Security Review, Gemini CLI, and Copilot Agent.
